rationale: Fidelity/deferral lens. The spec covers exactly its claimed wave-2 scope with nothing dropped or invented. DRY #3 (all six frontmatter sites), Perf #1/#4/#5, the trace.Event.MarshalJSON deletion, and the golangci carve-outs all map to requirements with line-number citations I verified against the branch base (event.go:53, plan.go:207/686/266, spec.go:270, impl.go:440, budgeter.go:581/614, state.go:189 all present). Out of Scope is complete against the report's full inventory: every DRY #2/#4/#6-#18 and Perf #2/#3/#6/#7 item is explicitly excluded or attributed to wave 1; nothing is silently omitted. The wave-1 deferral story matches spec 107's actual Out of Scope (trace + golangci deferred as unowned/non-doc, isDocFile rootOperatorDocs reasoning identical). The three-carve-out nuance is handled with more care than the report: two inert removed, findRoot deferred because it is still live on this pre-107 base (confirmed: findRoot present, unparam flags it). Trace's workflow ownership is grounded in the real import graph (verified: recording/otel import neither trace nor otel). Minor notes below, none blocking.
findings: VERIFIED: .golangci.yml has exactly four unparam carve-outs (brownfield/plan.go:77, isNeighbor:84, findRoot:88, validateReviewMode:92). R3 removes the two inert (brownfield, isNeighbor) and keeps findRoot+validateReviewMode — matches AC greps exactly.

VERIFIED: internal/trace importers are cmd/mindspec (root.go, trace.go), internal/instruct/run.go, internal/bead/bdcli.go — 2 of 3 packages workflow, matching R1's domain justification. recording and otel import neither trace nor otel, confirming the spec's rebuttal of the review's characterization.

VERIFIED base-state: findRoot present (next/beads.go:53), validate.SpecStatus live (frontmatter.go:22), readSpecApprovalStatus present (state.go:189), all R4/R5 re-impl sites at cited lines. 107-deletion targets (SpecStatusFromBytes, findRoot) still present, confirming 107 is unmerged into 108's base.

MINOR: The spec rebuts the review's 'used by recording/otel' characterization of trace, but that phrasing is not in the tracked review-source-report.md (which only calls MarshalJSON an 'aliased pass-through'). The rebuttal is empirically correct and the ownership decision is independently justified, so harmless — likely from the fuller subsystem-agent review not captured in the condensed report. No change required.

MINOR: R7 adds normalizeImpactedDomains (ownership_resolve.go:72) as a hoisting site beyond the report's named divergence.go/docsync.go sites. This completes the same Perf #1 fix consistently rather than inventing scope; acceptable.

MINOR (cosmetic): Out of Scope labels DRY #2 'setup override-flag validation dedup' — DRY #2 is actually cmd complete.go/impl.go override flags, not setup. The DRY #2 reference is explicit and correct; only the 'setup' word is a mislabel.

CROSS-SPEC NOTE (planning, not spec defect): both 107 and 108 touch internal/validate/plan.go — 107 deletes IsDomainCoveredCtx (:647), 108 edits parsePlanFrontmatter (:266) and coverageOf (:660+). Different functions, no semantic conflict, standard-merge overlap only. The load-bearing findRoot merge-order sequencing IS explicitly handled (rider on whichever of 107/108 merges last); this plan.go textual overlap is lesser and needs no spec change.
